perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Distribution Channels" means the distribution of the Licensed Products through GNC retail and online chains worldwide during the First Contract Year and, thereafter, through MusclePharm's other worldwide distribution channels, as mutually determined by MusclePharm and the AS Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Distribution Channels" means the distribution of the Licensed Products through channels determined solely by MusclePharm.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for mutual agreement on distribution channels after the First Contract Year, MusclePharm gains unilateral control. This contradicts section 12(a) which states MusclePharm will 'promote both the goodwill of the Endorser and the market reputation of the Licensed Products', as MusclePharm can now distribute through channels that may harm the Endorser's reputation without the AS Parties' consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) MusclePharm shall utilize its commercially reasonable efforts to advertise and promote the Licensed Products at its own expense and to sell the Licensed Products through the Distribution Channels worldwide as contemplated herein during the Term and to promote both the goodwill of the Endorser and the market reputation of the Licensed Products. MusclePharm will conduct its activities relating to the marketing of the Licensed Products in a professional manner. In that connection:
perturbation.justification: The original text required mutual agreement on distribution channels after the first year. The changed text gives MusclePharm unilateral control over distribution channels. This contradicts section 12(a), which requires MusclePharm to 'promote both the goodwill of the Endorser and the market reputation of the Licensed Products.' Unilateral control allows MusclePharm to choose distribution channels that could harm the Endorser's reputation, directly conflicting with this obligation. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction based on Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The AS Parties shall be entitled to approve the Marketing Plan, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed. MusclePharm shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to market and distribute the Licensed Products, and MusclePharm shall allocate between $3,000,000 and $5,000,000 in the First Contract Year and $5,000,000 in each subsequent Contract Year (including any renewal terms if any) toward the marketing of the Licensed Products (the "Marketing Budget"); it being understood that the parties may mutually decide not to deploy the full Marketing Budget in any Contract Year if in the good faith determination by the parties that the deployment of the full Marketing Budget is unnecessary to achieve its projected revenue targets in connection with the sale of the Licensed Products.
perturbation.changed_text: MusclePharm shall use its discretion to market and distribute the Licensed Products, allocating a budget as it deems appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the AS Parties' approval right for the Marketing Plan and eliminating the specific budget allocation, MusclePharm has complete control over marketing. This directly contradicts Section 13(a) which emphasizes the need to 'maintain the goodwill and integrity of the Endorser' and requires AS Parties approval of 'advertising campaigns, slogans, claims made and other Name and Appearance Rights associated with the Endorser IP'. Now MusclePharm can market in a way that damages the Endorser's goodwill without approval.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) MusclePharm acknowledges and agrees that, in order to maintain the goodwill and integrity of the Endorser, the Name and Appearance Rights, and the Trademarks (the "Endorser IP"), the Licensed Products shall be of a standard and of such style, appearance and quality as to protect and enhance the goodwill associated with the Endorser IP, which standard the AS Parties may from time to time prescribe and which, in any event, shall be of substantially the same or better quality than the samples previously provided by MusclePharm to Endorser. To this end, MusclePharm will use the approval form attached hereto as Exhibit "E" to obtain required approvals under this Agreement (including, ingredients contained in the Licensed Products). Prior to any use of any of the Endorser IP, MusclePharm shall submit to the AS Parties for the AS Parties' prior written approval all artwork, photos, images, writings, advertising campaigns, slogans, claims made and other Name and Appearance Rights associated with the Endorser IP, samples of materials and design of the Licensed Products on which the Endorser IP are to appear and of all advertising, press and promotional literature which MusclePharm intends to use in the marketing or merchandising of the Licensed Products using the submission form in Exhibit "E" attached hereto. The AS Parties shall respond to any such approval request within ten (10) business days. To the extent that the AS Parties shall fail to respond within such ten (10 day) period, the submissions shall be deemed disapproved. Should MusclePharm desire to submit the same request for approval, the AS Parties shall respond within five (5) days detailing the reason for disapproval. Should the AS Parties fail to respond in this last Five (5) business day period, submission shall be deemed approved. In addition, MusclePharm shall send, at its expense, at a minimum, two (2) representative samples of each Licensed Products, at each of the concept, pre-production and production stages, to the AS Parties at the address set forth in Section 20 below for prior approval. During the Term, MusclePharm will also send two (2) representative samples of the Licensed Products to the AS Parties at the address set forth in Section 20 below upon request so that the AS Parties can determine whether the quality of the Licensed Products bearing the Endorser IP is being maintained.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants MusclePharm complete discretion over marketing and budget allocation, directly contradicting Section 13(a), which requires AS Parties' approval for marketing campaigns, advertising, and other uses of the Endorser IP. The original text provided for AS Parties approval which protected the Endorser's goodwill and integrity, whereas now MusclePharm has no obligation to seek approval. The change eliminates the budget requirement which damages the Endorser's ability to project revenues, and potentially the Endorser's goodwill if the budget is drastically reduced.

perturbation.original_text: All cash payments shall be made via wire transfer to the Lender to an account provided by Lender or his representative.
perturbation.changed_text: All cash payments shall be made via methods deemed appropriate by MusclePharm.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the 'wire transfer to the Lender' clause, MusclePharm can choose any method for payment. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 7(a)(vi) states 'MusclePharm will send all statements and payments, including all royalties, to the Lender to the address set forth in Section 20 below. MusclePharm will make all payments payable to the Lender'. If MusclePharm chooses a method that prevents the Lender from receiving payment or makes it difficult, it breaches the intent and possibly the letter of section 7, even though that section remains unchanged.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (vi) MusclePharm will send all statements and payments, including all royalties, to the Lender to the address set forth in Section 20 below. MusclePharm will make all payments payable to the Lender.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows MusclePharm to use 'methods deemed appropriate', which contradicts the original text that mandates 'wire transfer' and section 7(a)(vi) which dictates that payments must be 'payable to the Lender' and sent to the address in Section 20. The altered clause enables methods that could potentially prevent the Lender from receiving payment as stated in the original contract. The altered text provides MusclePharm with a payment discretion that can directly conflict with Lender's payment guarantee in Section 7.
